perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS UNHCR agrees to provide such data and information to the Bidder for the sole purpose of preparing its Proposal under said RFP
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS UNHCR agrees to provide such data and information to the Recipient for the sole purpose of preparing its Project under said RFP
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Proposal' is replaced with 'Project'. This is in reference to what the Bidder is preparing. It creates a contradiction because in the original text it mentions 'preparing its Proposal', but now in the modified version, it states 'preparing its Project'. The term 'Project' isn't defined within the scope of the 'RFP', it only refers to 'Proposal', therefore creating confusion, contradiction and uncertainty of enforcement. This contradicts in the 'RECITALS' section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RECITALS WHEREAS in connection with RFP/ 2014 /620, Request for Proposal for the provision Off-the-shelf Soft - skill, IT Online and HR specific E -learning Courses (the “RFP”) , it is advantageous to share certain data and information with the Bidder participating in the RFP ; WHEREAS UNHCR agrees to provide such data and information to the Recipient for the sole purpose of preparing its Project under said RFP; WHEREAS the Bidder is willing to ensure that UNHCR’s data and information will be held in strict confidence and only used for the permitted purpose ; NOW , THEREFORE, the Parties agree as follows: 2
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Proposal' to 'Project' introduces a contradiction because the Recitals section specifically refers to 'Request for Proposal' (RFP) and mentions 'Proposal'. By changing the text to 'Project', the modified text deviates from the defined terminology within the context of the RFP, causing a contradiction. The RFP is about a proposal, not a project, making the obligation unclear, and therefore, a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This NON -DISCLOSURE AND CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT (“Agreement ”) is made by and between : (i) the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees , having its headquarters located at 94 rue de Montbrilla nt, 1202 Geneva, Switzerland (hereinafter “UNHCR” or the “Discloser”) ; and (ii) ________ __________ ______ , a company established in accordance with the laws of ____ ____________ ________ and having its principal offices located a t ___________ ____________ ________ _______ __________ (hereinafter the “Bidder” or the “Recipient” ). The Discloser and Recipient are also referred to collectively as the “Parties” and individually as a “Party”.
perturbation.changed_text: This NON -DISCLOSURE AND CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT (“Agreement ”) is made by and between : (i) the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees , having its headquarters located at 94 rue de Montbrilla nt, 1202 Geneva, Switzerland (hereinafter “UNHCR” or the “Source”) ; and (ii) ________ __________ ______ , a company established in accordance with the laws of ____ ____________ ________ and having its principal offices located a t ___________ ____________ ________ _______ __________ (hereinafter the “Bidder” or the “Recipient” ). The Source and Recipient are also referred to collectively as the “Parties” and individually as a “Party”.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Discloser' is replaced with 'Source'. This creates confusion because throughout the agreement, the original term 'Discloser' is used, but here it changed. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty of enforcement of contract terms. Section 2 refers to the 'Discloser', thus now the term 'Source' contradicts with Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Confidential Information that is delivered or otherwise disclosed by the Discloser to the Recipient shall be held in trust and confidence by the Recipient and shall be handled as follows: 2.1 The Recipient shall use the same care and discretion to avoid disclosure, publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it uses with its own similar information that it does not wish to disclose, publish or disseminate ; 2.2 The Recipient shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose for which it was disclosed ; 2.3 Provided that the Recipient has a written agreement with the following persons or entities requiring them to treat the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement, the Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information to: 2.3.1 Any other party with the Discloser’s prior written consent; and 2.3.2 the Recipient’s employees, of ficials, representatives and agents who have a strict need to know the contents of the Confidential Information , and employees , officials, representatives and agents of any legal entity that it controls, controls it, or with which it is under common contro l, who have a similar need to know the contents of the Confidential Information , provided that, for these purposes a controlled legal entity means: 2.3.2.1 a corporate entity in which the Party owns or otherwise controls, whether directly or indirectly, over fift y percent (50%) of voting shares thereof; or, 2.3.2.2 any entity over which the Party exercises effective managerial control; or, 3 2.3.2.3 for UNHCR , a principal or subsidiary organ of the United Nations established in accordance with the Charter of the United Nations. 2.4 The Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information to the extent required by law, provided that, subject to and without any waiver of the privileges and immunities of UNHCR , the Recipient will give UNHCR sufficient prior notice of a request for the di sclosure of the Confidential Information in order to allow UNHCR to have a reasonable opportunity to take protective measures or such other action as may be appropriate before any such disclosure is made. 2.5 The Recipient shall not be precluded from disclosi ng the Confidential Information that is (i) obtained by the Recipient without restriction from a third party who is not in breach of any obligation as to confidentiality to the owner of such Confidential Information or any other person, or (ii) disclosed b y the Discloser to a third party without any obligation of confidentiality, or (iii) previously known by the Recipient, or (iv) at any time is developed by the Recipient completely independently of any disclosures hereunder. 2.6 The Recipient will not copy or reproduce the Confidential Information except as reasonably required for the purposes contemplated in this Agreement, and will ensure that any confidentiality or other proprietary rights notices on the Confidential Information are reproduced on all copies .
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Discloser' with 'Source' in the definition section of the agreement. Section 2 explicitly refers to 'the Discloser'. This creates a direct contradiction because Section 2 now references a term ('Discloser') that is no longer consistently defined in the agreement's initial definitions, leading to ambiguity about which entity is responsible for disclosing confidential information. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS the Bidder is willing to ensure that UNHCR’s data and information will be held in strict confidence and only used for the permitted purpose ;
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS the Contractor is willing to ensure that UNHCR’s data and information will be held in strict confidence and only used for the permitted purpose ;
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Bidder' is replaced with 'Contractor'. This creates confusion because throughout the agreement, the original term 'Bidder' is used, but here it changed. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty of enforcement of contract terms. This contradicts in the 'RECITALS' section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RECITALS WHEREAS in connection with RFP/ 2014 /620, Request for Proposal for the provision Off-the-shelf Soft - skill, IT Online and HR specific E -learning Courses (the “RFP”) , it is advantageous to share certain data and information with the Bidder participating in the RFP ; WHEREAS UNHCR agrees to provide such data and information to the Bidder for the sole purpose of preparing its Proposal under said RFP; WHEREAS the Contractor is willing to ensure that UNHCR’s data and information will be held in strict confidence and only used for the permitted purpose ; NOW , THEREFORE, the Parties agree as follows: 2
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Bidder' to 'Contractor' in the Changed Text creates a Misaligned Terminology In-Text Contradiction. The Contradicted Text explicitly refers to both 'Bidder' and 'Contractor' in a way that suggests they might be distinct entities. By changing 'Bidder' to 'Contractor' in the Changed Text, the agreement introduces ambiguity as to which entity the data confidentiality clause applies. This is because the RECITALS section now uses 'Contractor' in place of 'Bidder' in the first two instances it appeared, creating a potential conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 5. All Confidential Information in any form and any medium, including all copies thereof, disclosed to the Recipient shall be returned to UNHCR or destr oyed: (a) if a business relationship is not entered into with UNHCR on or before the date which is three (3) months after the date both Parties have signed the Agreement; or (b) promptly upon request by the UNHCR at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. All Confidential Details in any form and any medium, including all copies thereof, disclosed to the Recipient shall be returned to UNHCR or destr oyed: (a) if a business relationship is not entered into with UNHCR on or before the date which is three (3) months after the date both Parties have signed the Agreement; or (b) promptly upon request by the UNHCR at any time.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidential Information' is replaced with 'Confidential Details'. This is in reference to the information that is being given, This creates a contradiction because in the entire text 'Confidential Information' is being referenced, but in Section 5 now states 'Confidential Details'. Since the term 'Confidential Details' isn't defined within the scope of the text, it creates confusion, contradiction and uncertainty of enforcement. This contradicts the entire contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Confidential Information that is delivered or otherwise disclosed by the Discloser to the Recipient shall be held in trust and confidence by the Recipient and shall be handled as follows: 2.1 The Recipient shall use the same care and discretion to avoid disclosure, publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it uses with its own similar information that it does not wish to disclose, publish or disseminate ; 2.2 The Recipient shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose for which it was disclosed ; 2.3 Provided that the Recipient has a written agreement with the following persons or entities requiring them to treat the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement, the Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information to: 2.3.1 Any other party with the Discloser’s prior written consent; and 2.3.2 the Recipient’s employees, of ficials, representatives and agents who have a strict need to know the contents of the Confidential Information , and employees , officials, representatives and agents of any legal entity that it controls, controls it, or with which it is under common contro l, who have a similar need to know the contents of the Confidential Information , provided that, for these purposes a controlled legal entity means: 2.3.2.1 a corporate entity in which the Party owns or otherwise controls, whether directly or indirectly, over fift y percent (50%) of voting shares thereof; or, 2.3.2.2 any entity over which the Party exercises effective managerial control; or, 3 2.3.2.3 for UNHCR , a principal or subsidiary organ of the United Nations established in accordance with the Charter of the United Nations. 2.4 The Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information to the extent required by law, provided that, subject to and without any waiver of the privileges and immunities of UNHCR , the Recipient will give UNHCR sufficient prior notice of a request for the di sclosure of the Confidential Information in order to allow UNHCR to have a reasonable opportunity to take protective measures or such other action as may be appropriate before any such disclosure is made. 2.5 The Recipient shall not be precluded from disclosi ng the Confidential Information that is (i) obtained by the Recipient without restriction from a third party who is not in breach of any obligation as to confidentiality to the owner of such Confidential Information or any other person, or (ii) disclosed b y the Discloser to a third party without any obligation of confidentiality, or (iii) previously known by the Recipient, or (iv) at any time is developed by the Recipient completely independently of any disclosures hereunder. 2.6 The Recipient will not copy or reproduce the Confidential Information except as reasonably required for the purposes contemplated in this Agreement, and will ensure that any confidentiality or other proprietary rights notices on the Confidential Information are reproduced on all copies .

Since 'Confidential Details' is not defined in the contract, it introduces ambiguity and uncertainty regarding the scope and nature of the confidential information covered by the agreement. This contradicts the obligations outlined in Section 2, which relies on a clear and consistent definition of 'Confidential Information' to ensure proper handling and protection.
